From a0fdca138feec2c2e94eb75ca1f8b76678b4d152 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Lukasz Lenart <lukaszlenart@apache.org>
Date: Tue, 14 Jun 2016 06:53:36 +0200
Subject: [PATCH] Improves validation RegEx


diff --git a/core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java b/core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
index f49d91468..fd678a70b 100644
--- a/core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
+++ b/core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
@@ -81,7 +81,7 @@ public class URLValidator extends FieldValidatorSupport {
                     "|((\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])\\.){3}" +
                     "(\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])" +
                     ")(:\\d+)?" +
-                    ")(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
+                    ")(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
                     "(\\?([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)" +
                     "?)?)?" +
                     "(#([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)?" +

diff --git a/core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java b/core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java
index 2895d801c..77223ebed 100644
--- a/core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java
+++ b/core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java
@@ -138,6 +138,7 @@ public class URLValidatorTest extends XWorkTestCase {
         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("").matches());
         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("   ").matches());
         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("no url").matches());
+		assertFalse(pattern.matcher("http://example.com////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////??").matches());
 
         assertTrue(pattern.matcher("http://www.opensymphony.com").matches());
         assertTrue(pattern.matcher("https://www.opensymphony.com").matches());
-- 
2.34.1

### Modifications in Each File

1. **URLValidator.java**
   - The regular expression used for URL validation has been modified. Specifically, the part of the regex that handles the path component of the URL has been changed from:
     ```
     "(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*"
     ```
     to:
     ```
     "(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*"
     ```
   - This change allows for an optional leading slash in the path component of the URL.

2. **URLValidatorTest.java**
   - A new test case has been added to validate the URL regex. The test checks that a URL with excessive slashes and question marks, specifically `"http://example.com////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////??"`, does not match the regex, ensuring that the updated regex correctly identifies invalid URLs.

### Relationships Between Modifications

- The modification in `URLValidator.java` enhances the URL validation logic by allowing for a more flexible path component in the regex. This change is directly related to the new test case added in `URLValidatorTest.java`, which aims to ensure that the updated regex behaves as expected by rejecting URLs that are improperly formatted, particularly those with excessive slashes and query indicators. The test serves to validate the effectiveness of the regex modification, ensuring that it meets the intended validation criteria.

2. output the file name that is most likely to cause the bug.

+++ b/core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
@@ -81,7 +81,7 @@ public class URLValidator extends FieldValidatorSupport {
                     "|((\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])\\.){3}" +
                     "(\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])" +
                     ")(:\\d+)?" +
-                    ")(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
+                    ")(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +

         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("").matches());
         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("   ").matches());
         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("no url").matches());
+		assertFalse(pattern.matcher("http://example.com////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////??").matches());
 
         assertTrue(pattern.matcher("http://www.opensymphony.com").matches());
         assertTrue(pattern.matcher("https://www.opensymphony.com").matches());

2.34.1

### Modifications in Each File

1. **URLValidator.java**
   - The regular expression used for URL validation has been modified. Specifically, the part of the regex that matches the path component of the URL has been changed from:
     ```
     "(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*"
     ```
     to:
     ```
     "(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*"
     ```
   - This change allows for an optional leading slash in the path component of the URL.

2. **URLValidatorTest.java**
   - A new test case has been added to check the behavior of the URL validation regex. The test asserts that a URL with excessive slashes and question marks, specifically `"http://example.com////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////??"`, does not match the regex, confirming that the validation is working as intended.

### Relationships Between Modifications

- The modification in `URLValidator.java` enhances the URL validation logic by allowing for a more flexible path component in the regex. This change is directly related to the new test case added in `URLValidatorTest.java`, which ensures that the updated regex correctly identifies invalid URLs that do not conform to expected patterns. The test serves to validate the effectiveness of the regex modification, ensuring that it behaves correctly under various input scenarios.

Improves validation RegEx

Here is the root cause:
The original regular expression in `URLValidator.java` did not account for an optional leading slash in the path component of the URL, which could lead to incorrect validation of certain URL formats, allowing invalid URLs to pass as valid.

Here is the patch:
      */
     public String getUrlRegex() {
         if (StringUtils.isNotEmpty(urlRegexExpression)) {
             return (String) parse(urlRegexExpression, String.class);
         } else if (StringUtils.isNotEmpty(urlRegex)) {
             return urlRegex;
         } else {
             return "^(https?|ftp):\\/\\/" +
                     "(([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;\\?&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})+" +
                     "(:([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;\\?&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})+)?" +
                     "@)?(#?" +
                     ")((([a-z0-9]\\.|[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]\\.)*" +
                     "[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]" +
                     "|((\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])\\.){3}" +
                     "(\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])" +
                     ")(:\\d+)?" +
-                    ")(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
+                    ")(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
                     "(\\?([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)" +
                     "?)?)?" +
                     "(#([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)?" +
                     "$";
         }
     }

The code statements that lead to the bug are:
```java
"(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
```
Reason:
The original regular expression did not account for an optional leading slash in the path component of the URL. This means that URLs with a path starting without a slash could be incorrectly validated as valid, allowing invalid URL formats to pass through.

The code statements that fix the bug are:
```java
"(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
```
Reason:
The updated regular expression introduces `\\/{0,1}`, which allows for an optional leading slash in the path component of the URL. This change ensures that URLs with or without a leading slash are correctly validated, thus preventing invalid URLs from being accepted.
